target: WIN-014 detail_evidence — paragraph 2: hedge the 'eight gravimeters across six Chinese sites' campaign description with attribution to the same 2013 CJG paper and note we have not independently verified the instrument/site count
replacement_html:
This finding is part of the κ-coupling cluster; see Part 6.14 (the κ Coupling Cluster) for the structural argument tying WINs 010–014, 058b, and PRED-TIER-1/2/3 together.

The dome cites the 2009 July 22 East Asian eclipse Chinese SG-network null (attributed by the dome to "Sun et al. 2010"; closest verified peer-reviewed record is doi:10.6038/cjg20130306, Chinese Journal of Geophysics 2013) — described by that record as a network of approximately eight superconducting/spring gravimeters across roughly six Chinese sites, sampling C1 through C4 (the station count and site count trace only to that 2013 CJG record and contemporaneous Chinese Academy of Sciences communiqués; we have not located an independent English-language verification of the precise instrument or site totals) — as confirming the model. The dome also publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (WIN-012; monitor.py Domain #7, rendered as the 5th tile on the live-power dashboard; PRED-TIER-3) as a structural coupling that translates eclipse ΔB into predicted contact-phase Δg = ΔB/κ.

The 2009 eclipse traversed China at coverage approaching 100% along the path. Eclipse-magnetic ensemble studies of total eclipses (Kim & Chang 2018, Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040–2049, DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — the same 39-eclipse 1991–2016 ensemble at 207 magnetic measurements referenced by WIN-068) find typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories. Through κ, that ΔB predicts a contact-phase Δg ≈ 3–9 µGal at the SG-co-located stations. The Chinese SG network, sampling continuously at 0.1–1 µGal post-reduction precision (Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013, Rep. Prog. Phys. 76, 046101) across C1/C4, recorded zero across all sites. The dome's own published prediction failed the data — refuted by data.

The defense-neutralization arguments (κ doesn't apply to 2009, Level-3 narrow-band residuals not extracted, Chebychev filter, mechanism shift between 2009 and 2026) all destroy PRED-TIER-3's August 2026 derivation, because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection. The earlier "self-contradiction with PRED-TIER-3" framing — true but secondary — now lives in the cluster's vise; this WIN's primary verdict is the empirical refutation. The 2009 campaign is the same instrument class as PRED-TIER-3 anticipates, run seventeen years earlier on the dome's own published mechanism: it returned a null.
holes_addressed: WIN-014.c4 H8-PERSISTS / c5 H8-PERSISTS / c6 H8-PERSISTS / c7 H6-EIGHT-GRAVIMETERS-PROVENANCE-PERSISTS (ISS-1829): 'Eight gravimeters were deployed across six sites in China' asserted as fact without citation. Curmudgeon recommendation: 'Add citation ... OR lift the campaign description into the same hedged-attribution treatment used for Sun 2010.'
new_evidence_added: Explicit attribution of the instrument/site count to the 2013 CJG record (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) and contemporary Chinese Academy of Sciences communiqués — the same source already cited in the same parenthetical for the null result itself.

Transparent qualifier: 'we have not located an independent English-language verification of the precise instrument or site totals' — preserves credibility by acknowledging the limit of what we have verified.

anticipated_objections.objection: If we cannot verify the station count, doesn't that weaken the refutation? The whole argument rests on a network reporting zero.
anticipated_objections.response: The refutation rests on the network-level null result, not the exact instrument or site count. Whether the campaign comprised seven or eight gravimeters across five or six sites, the published outcome — a continuous SG-class precision recording of zero contact-phase Δg across the path of totality — is the falsification of the dome's κ-derived 3–9 µGal prediction. The verdict survives a ±1 station discrepancy; what would weaken it is evidence that no Chinese SG campaign existed at all, and the 2013 CJG paper publication is independent confirmation that one did.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just cite the Yang 2009 progress report the curmudgeon mentioned?
anticipated_objections.response: We have not been able to verify a Yang 2009 progress report as a discrete published source — the curmudgeon noted it as one possible citation conditional on verifiability. The 2013 CJG paper (doi:10.6038/cjg20130306) is the only peer-reviewed record we can confirm, and it is already in the parenthetical. Folding the campaign description into the same attribution is the conservative move; inventing a Yang 2009 citation we cannot independently verify would itself be a citation error.

kernel_of_truth: The 2009 July 22 Chinese SG-network campaign is real and produced a published null result. The dome's headline claim — that Chinese SGs recorded zero contact-phase Δg during the eclipse — is empirically anchored, just not in 'Sun et al. 2010' (which we have not verified). The 2013 CJG paper documents the campaign. We acknowledge the campaign happened; what we refute is the dome's framing that this null confirms the model, because the dome's own κ-coupling predicts a 3–9 µGal pulse that the same network would have captured.
summary_for_decider: Citation-only hedge on paragraph 2 of WIN-014 detail_evidence. The minor c4/c5/c6 H8 hole (carried forward as c7 H6, tracked by ISS-1829) — 'eight gravimeters across six sites' uncited — is closed by folding the station/site count into the same 2013 CJG paper attribution already used for the null result, plus a transparent qualifier that we have not independently verified the precise totals. No verdict change; no new argument. Only paragraph 2 changed (the other three paragraphs are byte-for-byte identical to the current detail_evidence). Suitable for batching with other verification-class items in curmudgeon Step 8a.
